title: Select features against a held-out fold
description: Drive feature selection from validation-fold importance and stability, not from full-dataset correlations that overfit.
body: Use when pruning a wide feature set to a stable, deployable subset. Do not rank features by correlation on the full dataset and keep the top-k — that overfits selection to noise and can include leaky features. Actions: compute importance and stability across cross-validation folds, drop low-stability or redundant features, and confirm the pruned set holds metric on a fresh holdout. Validation signals include selection that is stable across folds and a holdout metric preserved after pruning. Risk boundary: selecting on leaked or test-contaminated signals inflates results; if a kept feature is unstable across folds, treat it as suspect and review before shipping.
